name: Tarragon Vinegar
recipeIngredient: 1 cup packed fresh tarragon leaves (plus sprigs for garnish, rinsed and spun dry)
1 tablespoon freeze-dried green peppercorns (cracked coarse) plus whole peppercorns for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the fresh tarragon leaves in a clean quart-sized glass jar and gently bruise them with a wooden spoon.
2. Add the cracked peppercorns to the jar along with the white-wine vinegar.
3. Cover the mixture with the lid and allow it to steep in a cool, dark place for 4 days to 2 weeks.
4. Strain the vinegar through a fine sieve into a clean glass pitcher, discarding the solids.
5. Pour the strained vinegar into two clean half-pint glass jars.
6. Add the tarragon sprigs and whole peppercorns to each jar.
7. Seal the jars with lids.
